 {"id":"caam-byup","title":"Implement monitor output renderers (table/brief/JSON)","description":"# Task: Implement monitor output renderers (table/brief/JSON)

## Overview
Create multiple output renderers for the monitor state, supporting different use cases from rich TUI displays to tmux status bar integration.

## Renderers

### Table Renderer (default)
Rich ASCII table for interactive terminal use:
```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    LIVE USAGE MONITOR                                  ║
║                    Last updated: 12:34:56                              ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  CLAUDE                                                                 ║
║  alice@gmail.com  🟢 ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42% │ resets 4h 12m │ ready  ║
║  bob@gmail.com    🟡 ██████████████░░░░░░ 71% │ resets 1h 45m │ ready  ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Brief Renderer
One-line summary for tmux/status bar:
```
claude:42%▲ codex:18%▲
```

### JSON Renderer
Machine-readable for scripting:
```json
{
  "updated_at": "2025-01-10T12:34:56Z",
  "profiles": {...}
}
```

### Alert Renderer
Alerts-only mode for logging:
```
[2025-01-10 12:34:56] ⚠️  claude/carol@gmail.com at 85%
[2025-01-10 12:35:12] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 95% - SWITCH RECOMMENDED
```

## Technical Details

### Renderer Interface
```go
type Renderer interface {
    Render(state *MonitorState) string
}

type TableRenderer struct {
    width int
    showEmoji bool
}

type BriefRenderer struct {
    separator string
}

type JSONRenderer struct {
    pretty bool
}

type AlertRenderer struct {
    threshold float64
    history   []*Alert
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Table renderer produces clean ASCII output
- [ ] Brief renderer fits in tmux status bar (<80 chars)
- [ ] JSON renderer produces valid, parseable JSON
- [ ] Alert renderer tracks alert history (no duplicates)
- [ ] All renderers handle edge cases (no profiles, all errors, etc.)

## Files to Create
- internal/monitor/render.go
- internal/monitor/render_table.go
- internal/monitor/render_brief.go
- internal/monitor/render_json.go
- internal/monitor/render_alert.go
- internal/monitor/render_test.go

 {"id":"caam-c2qt","title":"Implement caam monitor command","description":"# Task: Implement caam monitor command

## Overview
Create the caam monitor command that provides live usage monitoring with multiple output formats.

## Command Interface
```
Usage:
  caam monitor [flags]

Flags:
  -i, --interval duration   Refresh interval (default 30s)
  -p, --provider strings    Providers to monitor (default: all)
  -f, --format string       Output format: table, brief, json, alerts
  -a, --alert               Enable alert mode (log alerts to stderr)
  -t, --threshold float     Alert threshold percentage (default 80)
  -n, --notify              Enable desktop notifications
  -w, --webhook string      Webhook URL for alerts
  -1, --once                Fetch once and exit (no live monitoring)
```

## Usage Examples
```bash
# Interactive monitor
caam monitor

# tmux status bar integration
caam monitor --format brief --once

# Alert mode with desktop notifications
caam monitor --alert --notify --threshold 80

# JSON output for scripting
caam monitor --format json --once | jq ".profiles[]"

# Monitor specific provider
caam monitor --provider claude
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Live monitoring with configurable refresh
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work (r=refresh, q=quit)
- [ ] All output formats work correctly
- [ ] Desktop notifications on alert (optional)
- [ ] Webhook notifications on alert (optional)
- [ ] --once mode exits cleanly

## Files to Create
- cmd/caam/cmd/monitor.go
- cmd/caam/cmd/monitor_test.go

 {"id":"caam-cbm9","title":"Unit tests for daemon package (background service)","description":"# Daemon Package Unit Tests

## Current State
- internal/daemon: 52.3% coverage

## Test Requirements

### 1. Daemon Lifecycle Tests
- Start() initialization
- Run() main loop behavior
- Stop() graceful shutdown
- Context cancellation handling

### 2. PID File Tests
- WritePIDFile() atomic write
- ReadPIDFile() parsing
- RemovePIDFile() cleanup
- Stale PID detection
- IsProcessRunning() check

### 3. Config Loading Tests
- LoadConfig() from file
- Default values
- Invalid config handling

### 4. Refresh Scheduling Tests
- Profile scanning
- Expiry detection
- Refresh trigger timing
- Error recovery

### 5. Stats Collection Tests
- Stats() metrics
- Refresh counts
- Error tracking

### 6. Backup Scheduler Tests (new)
- ShouldBackup() timing
- NextBackupTime() calculation
- RotateBackups() behavior
- State persistence

## Implementation Notes
- Use short intervals for timing tests
- Mock time where needed for determinism
- Test concurrent access scenarios

## Files to Modify
- internal/daemon/daemon_test.go (expand)
- internal/daemon/backup_test.go (exists, verify coverage)","notes":"Increased coverage from 63.5% to 76.2%. Remaining uncovered code is mostly error paths for external system integration (auth providers, file system errors).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:03:21.072426689-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:25.042364576-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T20:23:25.042364576-05:00","close_reason":"Daemon coverage at 76.2% - improved from 52.3% baseline.  {"id":"caam-cedp","title":"Create internal/pricing package with provider pricing tables","description":"# Task: Create internal/pricing package with provider pricing tables

## Overview
Create a pricing package that contains up-to-date token pricing tables for all AI providers, enabling cost calculations and subscription value analysis.

## Background
Pay-per-token API pricing (as of 2025):

### Claude API
- claude-3-opus: $15/M input, $75/M output
- claude-3.5-sonnet: $3/M input, $15/M output
- claude-3-haiku: $0.25/M input, $1.25/M output
- claude-4-opus: TBD
- Cache read: 90% discount on input
- Cache creation: 25% premium on input

### OpenAI
- gpt-4o: $5/M input, $15/M output
- gpt-4o-mini: $0.15/M input, $0.60/M output

### Gemini
- gemini-ultra: $TBD
- gemini-pro: $TBD

## Technical Details

### Package Structure
```go
// internal/pricing/pricing.go

type TokenPrice struct {
    InputPer1M       float64
    OutputPer1M      float64
    CacheReadPer1M   float64  // Usually 10% of input
    CacheCreatePer1M float64  // Usually 125% of input
}

var ClaudePricing = map[string]TokenPrice{
    "claude-3-opus":      {15.00, 75.00, 1.50, 18.75},
    "claude-3.5-sonnet":  {3.00, 15.00, 0.30, 3.75},
    "claude-3-haiku":     {0.25, 1.25, 0.025, 0.3125},
    "claude-4-opus":      {TBD},
}

var OpenAIPricing = map[string]TokenPrice{...}
var GeminiPricing = map[string]TokenPrice{...}

// CalculateCost computes the total cost for token usage
func CalculateCost(usage *logs.TokenUsage, model, provider string) float64

// GetPrice looks up pricing for a model
func GetPrice(model, provider string) (*TokenPrice, bool)

// NormalizeModelName handles model name variations
func NormalizeModelName(model string) string
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Contains pricing for all major models
- [ ] Handles cache read/create token pricing
- [ ] Normalizes model name variants (e.g., "claude-3-opus-20240229" -> "claude-3-opus")
- [ ] Returns accurate cost calculations
- [ ] Easy to update when pricing changes

## Test Cases
1. Calculate cost for each supported model
2. Handle unknown model gracefully
3. Calculate with cache tokens
4. Verify normalization of model name variants

## Files to Create
- internal/pricing/pricing.go
- internal/pricing/claude.go
- internal/pricing/openai.go
- internal/pricing/gemini.go
- internal/pricing/pricing_test.go

 {"id":"caam-cfb","title":"Usage analytics CLI command","description":"## Purpose
Add `caam usage` command to display profile usage statistics from the command line.

## Background
Users should be able to see their usage patterns to understand which profiles
they use most, identify underutilized accounts, and optimize their workflow.

## Command Structure
```bash
# Summary view (default)
caam usage

# Detailed view for specific profile
caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed

# Time range filtering
caam usage --days 30
caam usage --since 2025-12-01

# Output formats
caam usage --json
caam usage --csv
```

## Example Output
```
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs
gemini/default       3 sessions    0.5 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work --detailed
Session History for claude/work
───────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
...

Total: 42 sessions, 18.5 hours over 7 days
Average session: 26 minutes
Most active day: Monday (12 sessions)
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// cmd/caam/usage.go
var usageCmd = &cobra.Command{
    Use:   "usage",
    Short: "Display profile usage statistics",
    RunE:  runUsage,
}

func init() {
    usageCmd.Flags().StringP("profile", "p", "", "Profile to show (provider/name)")
    usageCmd.Flags().Bool("detailed", false, "Show detailed session history")
    usageCmd.Flags().Int("days", 7, "Number of days to include")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("since", "", "Start date (YYYY-MM-DD)")
    usageCmd.Flags().String("format", "table", "Output format: table, json, csv")
}

func runUsage(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    db, err := db.Open()
    if err != nil {
        return err
    }
    defer db.Close()
    
    // Query and format based on flags
}
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- cmd/caam/usage.go
- cmd/caam/usage_test.go
- Modify cmd/caam/root.go to add subcommand

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Summary shows all profiles with session count and duration
- [ ] Detailed view shows individual sessions for a profile
- [ ] Time range filtering works correctly
- [ ] JSON and CSV export work
- [ ] Handles empty database gracefully
- [ ] Performance acceptable for large activity logs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:22:53.646488393-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:11:12.338404628-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam usage command with summary/detailed views and json/csv output.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-cfb","depends_on_id":"caam-r36","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:28:57.057526979-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":21,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting caam usage command: implement cmd/caam/cmd/usage.go with summary + detailed profile view, since/days filtering, and table/json/csv output using internal/db.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:06:53Z"},{"id":22,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented  command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:10:45Z"},{"id":23,"issue_id":"caam-cfb","author":"ubuntu","text":"(Follow-up) Implemented the caam usage command with summary + per-profile detailed session history, days/since filtering, and table/json/csv output backed by SQLite activity_log. Added tests for empty DB, summary json, and detailed json.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:11:02Z"}]}
